text: Managing a team effectively requires a balance of several key skills. First, clear communication is essential so that everyone understands their tasks and goals. Equally important is delegation: assign work according to each person’s strengths, but avoid overloading individuals. Regular feedback helps team members improve and feel valued, so offer both praise and constructive criticism. Motivation can be boosted by recognizing achievements and creating a positive work environment. Finally, when conflicts arise, address them quickly and fairly, listening to all sides to find a solution. By combining these practices, a manager can build a productive and harmonious team.
